Emerging Harbors, , , provided that:Pub. L. 118–272, div. A, title I, § 1129138 Stat. 3018Jan. 4, 2025“Not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary [of the Army] shall—Jan. 4, 2025“(1)issue guidance for the purpose of carrying out section 210(c)(3)(B) of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 (); and33 U.S.C. 2238(c)(3)(B)“(2)develop a mechanism to accept the non-Federal share of funds from a non-Federal interest for maintenance dredging carried out under such section.”

Annual Report on Harbor Maintenance Needs and Trust Fund Expenditures–(c), , , 3077, provided that:Pub. L. 118–272, div. A, title II, § 1206(a)138 Stat. 3076Jan. 4, 2025“(a).—In GeneralOn the date on which the budget of the President is submitted to Congress pursuant to , United States Code, for fiscal year 2026, and for each fiscal year thereafter, the Secretary [of the Army] shall submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate a report describing—section 1105 of title 31“(1)with respect to the fiscal year for which the budget is submitted, the operation and maintenance costs associated with harbors and inland harbors described in section 210(a)(2) of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 (), including a description of the costs required to achieve and maintain the constructed width and depth for such harbors and inland harbors and the costs for expanded uses at eligible harbors and inland harbors (as defined in section 210(d)(2) of such Act []), on a project-by-project basis;33 U.S.C. 2238(a)(2)33 U.S.C. 2238(d)(2)“(2)as of the date on which the report is submitted, expenditures and deposits into the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund established under section 9505 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [];26 U.S.C. 9505“(3)an identification of the amount of funding requested in the budget of the President for the operation and maintenance costs associated with such harbors and inland harbors, on a project-by-project basis;“(4)an explanation of how the amount of funding described in paragraph (2) complies with the requirements of section 102 [probably should be “102(a)”] of the Water Resources Development Act of 2020 [div. AA of ] ( note);Pub. L. 116–26033 U.S.C. 2238“(5)an identification of the unmet operation and maintenance needs associated with such harbors and inland harbors, on a project-by-project basis, that remains after accounting for the amount identified under paragraph (3); and“(6)a description of deposits made into the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund in the fiscal year preceding the fiscal year of the applicable budget submission and the sources of such deposits.“(b).—Additional RequirementIn the first report required to be submitted under subsection (a), the Secretary shall identify, to the maximum extent practicable, transportation cost savings realized by achieving and maintaining the constructed width and depth for the harbors and inland harbors described in section 210(a)(2) of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 [], on a project-by-project basis.33 U.S.C. 2238(a)(2)“(c).—Public AvailabilityThe Secretary shall make the report submitted under subsection (a) publicly available, including on a publicly available website.”

Criteria for Funding Operation and Maintenance of Small, Remote, and Subsistence Harbors, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–263, div. H, title LXXXI, § 8131136 Stat. 3718Dec. 23, 2022“(a).—In GeneralNot later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary [of the Army] shall develop specific criteria for the annual evaluation and ranking of maintenance dredging requirements for small harbors and remote and subsistence harbors, taking into account the following:Dec. 23, 2022“(1)The contribution of a harbor to the local and regional economy.“(2)The extent to which a harbor has deteriorated since the last cycle of maintenance dredging.“(3)Public safety concerns.“(b).—Inclusion in GuidanceThe Secretary shall include the criteria developed under subsection (a) in the annual Civil Works Direct Program Development Policy Guidance of the Secretary.“(c).—Report to CongressThe Secretary shall include in each biennial report submitted under section 210(e)(3) of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 ([former] ) a ranking of projects in accordance with the criteria developed under subsection (a) of this section.33 U.S.C. 2238(e)(3)“(d).—DefinitionsIn this section:“(1).—Remote and subsistence harborThe term ‘remote and subsistence harbor’ means a harbor with respect to which section 2006 of the Water Resources Development Act of 2007 () applies, as determined by the Secretary [of the Army].33 U.S.C. 2242“(2).—Small harborThe term ‘small harbor’ includes an emerging harbor, as such term is defined in section 210 of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 ().”33 U.S.C. 2238

Authorization of Appropriations for Navigation, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–260, div. AA, title I, § 102(a)134 Stat. 2618Dec. 27, 2020“(1).—In generalIn carrying out subsection (c) of section 210 of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 (), for each fiscal year, of the funds made available under such section (including funds appropriated from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund), the Secretary [of the Army] shall, to the extent practicable, unless otherwise directed in an Act making appropriations for the Corps of Engineers, make expenditures to pay for operation and maintenance costs of the harbors and inland harbors referred to in subsection (a)(2) of such section, to the extent there are identifiable operations and maintenance needs, of—33 U.S.C. 2238“(A)not less than 15 percent of such funds for emerging harbor projects, including eligible breakwater and jetty needs at such harbor projects;“(B)not less than 13 percent of such funds for projects that are located within the Great Lakes Navigation System;“(C)12 percent of such funds for expanded uses carried out at donor ports and energy transfer ports, of which—“(i)⅓ shall be provided to energy transfer ports; and“(ii)⅔ shall be provided to donor ports;“(D)not less than 17 percent of such funds for projects that are assigned to commercial strategic seaports; and“(E)any remaining funds for operation and maintenance costs of any harbor or inland harbor referred to in such subsection (a)(2) [] based on an equitable allocation of such funds among such harbors and inland harbors, in accordance with subsection (c)(1) of such section 210 [].33 U.S.C. 2238(a)(2)33 U.S.C. 2238(c)(1)“(2).—DefinitionsIn this subsection:“(A).—Commercial strategic seaportThe term ‘commercial strategic seaport’ means a commercial harbor supporting the coordination of efficient port operations during peacetime and national defense emergencies that is designated as strategic through the National Port Readiness Network.“(B).—Donor port; energy transfer portThe terms ‘donor port’ and ‘energy transfer port’ have the meanings given those terms in section 2106 of the Water Resources Reform and Development Act of 2014 ().33 U.S.C. 2238c“(C).—Emerging harbor project; great lakes navigation systemThe terms ‘emerging harbor project’ and ‘Great Lakes Navigation System’ have the meanings given those terms in section 210 of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 ().33 U.S.C. 2238“(3).—Effective dateThis subsection shall take effect on .”October 1, 2022

L. 114–322, title I, § 1112130 Stat. 1637Dec. 16, 2016“Not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of the Water Resources Development Act of 2016 [], the Secretary [of the Army] shall publish on the website of the Corps of Engineers guidance on the implementation of this section [amending this section, , and , Internal Revenue Code] and the amendments made by this section.”Dec. 16, 2016section 2211 of this titlesection 9505 of Title 26

children.text: 100 percent of the eligible operations and maintenance costs of those portions of the Saint Lawrence Seaway operated and maintained by the Great Lakes St. Lawrence Seaway Development Corporation for such fiscal year; and
up to 100 percent of the eligible operations and maintenance costs assigned to commercial navigation of all harbors and inland harbors within the United States.

children.heading: General fund
children.label: b General fund
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated out of the general fund of the Treasury of the United States for each fiscal year such sums as may be necessary to pay the balance of all eligible operations and maintenance costs not provided by payments from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund under this section.

children.heading: Operation and maintenance of harbor projects
children.label: c Operation and maintenance of harbor projects

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall make expenditures to pay for operation and maintenance costs of the harbors and inland harbors referred to in subsection (a)(2), including expenditures of funds appropriated from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund, based on an equitable allocation of funds among all such harbors and inland harbors.

children.label: i
children.text: consider the information obtained in the assessment conducted under subsection (e);

children.label: ii
children.text: consider the national and regional significance of harbor operations and maintenance; and

children.label: iii
children.text: as appropriate, consider national security and military readiness needs.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall not allocate funds under paragraph (1) based solely on the tonnage transiting through a harbor.

children.heading: Allocation
children.label: A Allocation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection, in making expenditures under paragraph (1) for each fiscal year, the Secretary shall allocate for operation and maintenance costs of emerging harbor projects an amount that is not less than 10 percent of the funds made available under this section for fiscal year 2012 to pay the costs described in subsection (a)(2).

children.heading: Additional uses at emerging harbors
children.label: B Additional uses at emerging harbors

children.heading: Uses
children.label: i Uses
children.text: In each fiscal year, the Secretary may use not more than $5,000,000 of funds allocated for emerging harbor projects under paragraph (1) to pay for the costs of up to 10 projects for maintenance dredging of a marina or berthing area, in an emerging harbor, that includes an area that is located adjacent to, or is accessible by, a Federal navigation project, subject to clauses (ii) and (iii) of this subparagraph.

children.label: I
children.text: supports commercial activities, including commercial fishing operations, commercial fish processing operations, recreational and sport fishing, and commercial boat yards; or

children.label: II
children.text: supports activities of the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating.

children.heading: Cost-sharing requirements
children.label: iii Cost-sharing requirements
children.text: The Secretary shall require a non-Federal interest to contribute not less than 25 percent of the costs for maintenance dredging of that portion of a maintenance dredging project described in clause (i) that is located outside of the Federal navigation project, which may be provided as an in-kind contribution, including through the use of dredge equipment owned by non-Federal interest to carry out such activities.

children.heading: Management of Great Lakes Navigation System
children.label: 4 Management of Great Lakes Navigation System
children.text: To sustain effective and efficient operation and maintenance of the Great Lakes Navigation System, including any navigation feature in the Great Lakes that is a Federal responsibility with respect to operation and maintenance, the Secretary shall manage all of the individually authorized projects in the Great Lakes Navigation System as components of a single, comprehensive system, recognizing the interdependence of the projects.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For each fiscal year, if priority funds are available, the Secretary shall use at least 10 percent of such funds for emerging harbor projects.

children.label: i
children.text: not less than 5 percent of such funds for underserved harbor projects; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not less than 10 percent of such funds for projects that are located within the Great Lakes Navigation System.

children.label: i
children.text: the total quantity of commerce supported by the water body on which the project is located; and

children.label: I
children.text: would be necessary at the underserved harbor project to provide sufficient clearance for fully loaded commercial vessels using the underserved harbor project to maneuver safely; and

children.label: II
children.text: does not exceed the constructed width and depth of the authorized navigation project.

children.heading: Definition of eligible harbor or inland harbor defined
children.label: A Definition of eligible harbor or inland harbor defined
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “eligible harbor or inland harbor” means a harbor or inland harbor at which the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes collected in the immediately preceding 3 fiscal years exceeds the value of the work carried out for the harbor or inland harbor using amounts from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund during those 3 fiscal years.

children.heading: Use of expanded uses funds
children.label: B Use of expanded uses funds

children.heading: Fiscal years 2015 through 2024
children.label: i Fiscal years 2015 through 2024
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2015 through 2024, of the priority funds available, the Secretary shall use not less than 10 percent of such funds for expanded uses carried out at an eligible harbor or inland harbor.

children.heading: Subsequent fiscal years
children.label: ii Subsequent fiscal years
children.text: For fiscal year 2025 and each fiscal year thereafter, the Secretary shall use not less than 10 percent of the priority funds available for expanded uses carried out at an eligible harbor or inland harbor.

children.label: i
children.text: the total amount of funding made available for projects at that eligible harbor or inland harbor from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund in the immediately preceding 3 fiscal years; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes collected at that harbor or inland harbor in the immediately preceding 3 fiscal years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2015 through 2024, if after fully funding all projects eligible for funding under paragraphs (1)(B) and (2)(B)(i), priority funds made available under those paragraphs remain unobligated, the Secretary shall use those remaining funds to pay for operation and maintenance costs of any harbor or inland harbor referred to in subsection (a)(2) based on an equitable allocation of those funds among the harbors and inland harbors.

children.label: i
children.text: use the criteria specified in subsection (c)(2)(A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: make amounts available in accordance with the requirements of paragraph (1)(A).

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary determines that the action is necessary to address the navigation needs of a harbor or inland harbor where safe navigation has been severely restricted due to an unforeseen event; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary provides within 90 days of the action notice and information on the need for the action to the Committee on Environment and Public Works and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Assessment of harbors and inland harbors
children.label: e Assessment of harbors and inland harbors

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 270 days after , and biennially thereafter, the Secretary shall assess, and issue a report to Congress on, the operation and maintenance needs and uses of the harbors and inland harbors referred to in subsection (a)(2).June 10, 2014

children.heading: Assessment of harbor needs and activities
children.label: 2 Assessment of harbor needs and activities

children.heading: Total operation and maintenance needs of harbors
children.label: A Total operation and maintenance needs of harbors
i
children.text: the total future costs required to achieve and maintain the constructed width and depth for the harbors and inland harbors referred to in subsection (a)(2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the total expected costs for uses described in subsection (c)(3)(B) and expanded uses at eligible harbors or inland harbors referred to in subsection (d)(2).

children.heading: Uses of harbors and inland harbors
children.label: B Uses of harbors and inland harbors
i
children.text: commercial navigation, including the movement of goods;

children.label: ii
children.text: domestic trade;

children.label: iii
children.text: international trade;

children.label: iv
children.text: commercial fishing;

children.label: v
children.text: subsistence, including use by Indian tribes (as defined in ) for subsistence and ceremonial purposes;section 5304 of title 25

children.label: vi
children.text: use as a harbor of refuge;

children.label: vii
children.text: transportation of persons;

children.label: viii
children.text: purposes relating to domestic energy production, including the fabrication, servicing, or supply of domestic offshore energy production facilities;

children.label: ix
children.text: activities of the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating;

children.label: x
children.text: activities of the Secretary of the Navy;

children.label: xi
children.text: public health and safety related equipment for responding to coastal and inland emergencies;

children.label: xii
children.text: recreation purposes; and

children.label: xiii
children.text: other authorized purposes.

children.heading: Opportunities for beneficial use of dredged materials
children.label: C Opportunities for beneficial use of dredged materials
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), the Secretary shall identify potential opportunities for the beneficial use of dredged materials obtained from harbors and inland harbors referred to in subsection (a)(2), including projects eligible under section 1122 of the Water Resources Development Act of 2016 (; note ).130 Stat. 164533 U.S.C. 23261See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Constructed width and depth
children.label: 1 Constructed width and depth
children.text: The term “constructed width and depth” means the width and depth to which a project has been constructed, which may not exceed the authorized width and depth of the project.

children.heading: Emerging harbor
children.label: 2 Emerging harbor
children.text: The term “emerging harbor” means a harbor or inland harbor referred to in subsection (a)(2) that transits less than 1,000,000 tons of cargo annually.

children.heading: Emerging harbor project
children.label: 3 Emerging harbor project
children.text: The term “emerging harbor project” means a project that is assigned to an emerging harbor.

children.label: A
children.text: The maintenance dredging of a berth in a harbor that is accessible to a Federal navigation project and that benefits commercial navigation at the harbor.

children.label: i
children.text: such dredging and disposal benefits commercial navigation at the harbor; and

children.label: ii
children.text: such sediment is located in and affects the maintenance of a Federal navigation project or is located in a berth that is accessible to a Federal navigation project.

children.label: i
children.text: is for the seismic reinforcement of a wharf or other berthing structure, or the repair or replacement of a deteriorating wharf or other berthing structure, at a port facility;

children.label: ii
children.text: benefits commercial navigation at the harbor; and

children.label: iii
children.text: is located in, or adjacent to, a berth that is accessible to a Federal navigation project.

children.label: D
children.text: An activity to maintain slope stability at a berth in a harbor that is accessible to a Federal navigation project if such activity benefits commercial navigation at the harbor.

children.label: i
children.text: Lake Superior;

children.label: ii
children.text: Lake Huron;

children.label: iii
children.text: Lake Michigan;

children.label: iv
children.text: Lake Erie; and

children.label: v
children.text: Lake Ontario;

children.label: B
children.text: all connecting waters between the lakes referred to in subparagraph (A) used for commercial navigation;

children.label: C
children.text: any navigation features in the lakes referred to in subparagraph (A) or waters described in subparagraph (B) that are a Federal operation or maintenance responsibility; and

children.label: D
children.text: areas of the Saint Lawrence River that are operated or maintained by the Federal Government for commercial navigation.

children.heading: Harbor maintenance tax
children.label: 6 Harbor maintenance tax
children.text: The term “harbor maintenance tax” means the amounts collected under .section 4461 of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: more than 1,000,000 tons of cargo; but

children.label: B
children.text: less than 10,000,000 tons of cargo.

children.label: A
children.text: the total funds that are made available under this section to pay the costs described in subsection (a)(2) for a fiscal year; and

children.label: B
children.text: the total funds made available under this section to pay the costs described in subsection (a)(2) in fiscal year 2012.

children.label: i
children.text: that is a moderate-use harbor project or an emerging harbor project;

children.label: ii
children.text: that has been maintained at less than the constructed width and depth of the project during each of the preceding 6 fiscal years; and

children.label: iii
children.text: for which State and local investments in infrastructure have been made at those projects during the preceding 6 fiscal years.

children.heading: Administration
children.label: B Administration
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, State and local investments in infrastructure shall include infrastructure investments made using amounts made available for activities under .section 5305(a)(9) of title 42
